title: Will Gets A Job

jokes.text: Thirty seconds on high, Spock. Energize.
jokes.setup: Will is using a microwave but addressing it as if commanding the Starship Enterprise
jokes.punchline: The mixing of Star Trek command language with mundane kitchen appliance use
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Will's playful personality while doing something mundane. Economy is excellent - gets the laugh immediately.

jokes.text: Captain, we're going into a warp drive, we're running low on dilithium crystals.
jokes.setup: Carlton joins Will's Star Trek roleplay with the microwave
jokes.punchline: Carlton earnestly playing along with Will's ridiculous premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his earnest participation in Will's absurd game shows their dynamic beautifully.

jokes.text: Do something, Bones. / Jim, I'm a doctor, damn it, not a short-order cook.
jokes.setup: Continuing Star Trek roleplay, Will calls on 'Bones' to help
jokes.punchline: Carlton adapts McCoy's famous catchphrase to their kitchen context
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay adapting a famous Star Trek line. The 'short-order cook' twist is clever and perfectly fits the kitchen setting.

jokes.text: Do I look like Wilma Flintstone?
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests using the regular oven instead of microwave
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated comparison to a Stone Age housewife
jokes.explanation: Functional pop culture reference joke. Gets a laugh but relies on recognition rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: Come on, beat it, man. There's barely enough for me.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks to share Will's food
jokes.punchline: Will's selfish refusal despite having plenty
jokes.explanation: Basic character beat showing Will's selfishness, but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: This one's Geoffrey's. He filled out the order form.
jokes.setup: Will accuses Carlton of having a Victoria's Secret catalog
jokes.punchline: Deflection that it belongs to the butler, who actually ordered from it
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - starts as Carlton being caught, pivots to Geoffrey being the real customer.

jokes.text: No, but if there's a God in heaven I will by Christmas.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Geoffrey knows a woman with 46D cup size
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's hopeful prayer delivered in his dry butler manner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line - dry British delivery with surprisingly randy content. Great character integration.

jokes.text: There's still the tux, the limousine... the pre-party party, the post-party party, and, in your case, bail.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing expensive homecoming costs
jokes.punchline: The escalating list ending with bail specifically for Will
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with perfect character-specific punchline. Carlton would definitely think Will needs bail money.

jokes.text: I always find that when you ask for large sums, it helps if you take his hand... look deep into his eyes, and tell him you have cramps.
jokes.setup: Hilary offering advice on getting money from Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous suggestion to fake menstrual cramps
jokes.explanation: Perfectly vapid Hilary advice - she would absolutely think this is sophisticated manipulation.

jokes.text: That only worked for Hilary. When I tried it he sent me to a specialist.
jokes.setup: Carlton reveals he actually tried Hilary's cramps advice
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's logical response to Carlton claiming cramps
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes the previous joke even funnier. Carlton actually trying it is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, that's only worth $25. Besides, it's really hard on your knees.
jokes.setup: Will suggests getting on hands and knees to beg
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical assessment of begging's monetary value and physical cost
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Carlton would absolutely have calculated the cost-benefit of different begging techniques.

jokes.text: And that suit you got on... man, it's great how it accentuates your massive... weight loss, man.
jokes.setup: Will trying to compliment Uncle Phil's appearance
jokes.punchline: Nearly calling Uncle Phil massive before awkward save
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with awkward recovery that feels natural for Will trying to be smooth.

jokes.text: I got cramps.
jokes.setup: Will has tried all the suggested tactics and finally resorts to the cramps line
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of Will, a teenage boy, claiming menstrual cramps
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution. Two words that land huge because of the setup. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: How much? What's it cost? And will I get sued?
jokes.setup: Will says he has cramps
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's immediate lawyer-like response covering all bases
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil reaction - practical, lawyer-minded, and shows he's been through this before.

jokes.text: I could close my eyes, and I'd swear I'm talking to Carlton.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil tells Will he's become like one of his own kids
jokes.punchline: Specifically comparing Will to Carlton, the most un-Will-like person
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main conflict. The comparison is perfectly designed to upset Will.

jokes.text: Anywhere. When the lava flows, it's every missionary for himself.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking where to put missionaries in her volcano project
jokes.punchline: Ashley's casual acceptance of missionary doom with twisted common saying
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark humor from young Ashley. Good twist on 'every man for himself.'

jokes.text: Where? I don't see a buffed guy. I only see Carlton.
jokes.setup: Tina comments on a buffed guy in a swimsuit
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent yet devastating assessment that Carlton isn't buffed
jokes.explanation: Brutal innocent delivery from Ashley. The matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, God. He's coming over here. I can't look. Hand me a missionary.
jokes.setup: Carlton approaches while Ashley is mortified
jokes.punchline: Ashley using volcano project missionaries as a shield from embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the missionaries, using them for a different comedic purpose.

jokes.text: You're athletic, too? I should have known by your bulging forearms.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions table tennis as his athletic activity
jokes.punchline: Tina's genuine amazement at Carlton's 'bulging forearms' from ping pong
jokes.explanation: Shows Tina's genuine infatuation making her see athleticism where none exists. Mild but effective.

jokes.text: They do draw stares.
jokes.setup: Tina comments on Carlton's 'bulging forearms'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's completely serious agreement about his non-existent muscles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton delusion delivered with complete confidence. His obliviousness is the joke.

jokes.text: I'm sure when he called you a sophomoric cretin it was just a figure of speech.
jokes.setup: Will is upset about what Uncle Phil said
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey assumes Will is upset about being called a cretin, not about being compared to Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Geoffrey's dry wit and assumption about what would actually bother Will.

jokes.text: Could you put that on a croissant for me? / Certainly.
jokes.setup: Will is ranting about not being bourgeois while Geoffrey makes him a sandwich
jokes.punchline: Will unconsciously requesting fancy bread while denying he's changed
jokes.explanation: Excellent subtle comedy showing Will's unconscious assimilation. The hypocrisy is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Swiss? / No, Gorgonzola.
jokes.setup: Continuing the sandwich preparation
jokes.punchline: Will requesting even fancier cheese while still denying he's bourgeois
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the unconscious bourgeois behavior. Gorgonzola is even fancier than Swiss.

jokes.text: Yo, man, how you gonna play a brother on the Grey Poupon?
jokes.setup: Will realizes Geoffrey gave Carlton fancy mustard but not him
jokes.punchline: Will's street language complaint about not getting the fancy condiment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will using street vernacular to complain about not getting fancy mustard shows his internal conflict.

jokes.text: Can you make me a Gorgonzola sandwich on a croissant, heavy on the Grey Poupon? / Certainly.
jokes.setup: Will just realized he's become like Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately orders the exact same fancy sandwich Will just ordered
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing that drives home the point. Carlton ordering identical sandwich confirms Will's fear.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Uncle Phil is right, G. I'm turning into Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton just ordered the identical fancy sandwich
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified self-awareness moment
jokes.explanation: Necessary story beat that pays off the running gag. Not hilarious but serves the narrative.

jokes.text: Now you see it, now you don't.
jokes.setup: Will dramatically rejecting Uncle Phil's check
jokes.punchline: Magician-style reveal while tearing up money
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment with Will's dramatic flair. The magic trick presentation adds style to his defiant gesture.

jokes.text: Yo, the funky Fresh is back in the flesh with a vengeance, homes.
jokes.setup: Will declares he's rejecting bourgeois life
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated street persona declaration
jokes.explanation: Shows Will overcompensating, but the line itself isn't particularly funny - more setup for what follows.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm gonna eat that though. / But I ain't gonna enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Will rejects the fancy sandwich
jokes.punchline: Will admits he'll eat it anyway but won't enjoy it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's internal conflict. The contradiction is very relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Excuse me, what did you call me? / I called you Will. / No, you didn't. You called me Carlton.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil calls Will by his name
jokes.punchline: Will's paranoid insistence that he was called Carlton
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's oversensitivity about the Carlton comparison. Character-driven but not exceptionally funny.

jokes.text: And they didn't, like, stop the game?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions running out of pasta salad in VIP section
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic suggestion that they should stop the game for pasta salad
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response highlighting the absurdity of Carlton's priorities.

jokes.text: I want to sit in the $2 seats where my feet be sticking to the floor and a big, fat, crusty, bloated dude is sitting behind me belching in my ear.
jokes.setup: Will explaining how he wants to experience basketball
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of authentic 'street' basketball experience
jokes.explanation: Good contrast comedy. Will's vivid description of 'authentic' experience vs. luxury shows his overcompensation.

jokes.text: Do you think they're gonna have that great pasta salad?
jokes.setup: Cindy arrives excited about the basketball game
jokes.punchline: Her immediate concern about pasta salad, proving Will's point about bourgeois priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - immediately proves Will's point about everyone being obsessed with VIP amenities.

jokes.text: Man, you take bad news better than anybody I ever met.
jokes.setup: Cindy's enthusiastic response to his homecoming news
jokes.punchline: Will misreading her excitement as acceptance of cancellation
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's obliviousness to Cindy's actual reaction. The miscommunication creates awkward comedy.

jokes.text: I wonder if it shows too much cleavage. / About how many corsages you gonna be needing?
jokes.setup: Cindy wondering if her dress is too revealing
jokes.punchline: Will's completely unrelated question about corsages
jokes.explanation: Shows Will being completely distracted by mention of cleavage. Basic teenage boy humor.

jokes.text: Carlton, those khaki pants make you look so Field and Stream. Take me camping.
jokes.setup: Tina sees Carlton in regular clothes
jokes.punchline: Her random association with outdoor magazine and camping request
jokes.explanation: Shows Tina's bizarre infatuation making her see adventure in Carlton's mundane outfit.

jokes.text: Well, I don't think anyone believed you were Colin Powell.
jokes.setup: Tina apologized for trying to sound official when paging Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dry observation about her failed impersonation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. The specific choice of Colin Powell makes it funnier than a generic authority figure.

jokes.text: Studying? That is so cool. Are you going to highlight or underline?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions going to study
jokes.punchline: Tina's genuine fascination with mundane study techniques
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tina character moment - finding the most boring things exciting because Carlton does them.

jokes.text: I think those two are a match made in midget heaven.
jokes.setup: Will observing Carlton and Tina together
jokes.punchline: Will's comment on their short stature compatibility
jokes.explanation: Basic height joke. Gets a laugh but relies on physical appearance rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Yeah, right, and I can't go, either. A dog ate my volcano.
jokes.setup: Will claims he can't come to Ashley's science fair because he has a job
jokes.punchline: Ashley's twist on 'dog ate my homework' excuse with her volcano project
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on a classic excuse. Ashley's intelligence and wit shine through with this creative variation.

jokes.text: You couldn't lie to Daddy about his weight, either?
jokes.setup: Ashley connecting Will's job to his inability to manipulate Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier weight loss compliment attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting Will's current situation to his earlier failed manipulation attempt.

jokes.text: Now, I want you to walk out that door, hop on your bike, and pedal like hell.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to get rid of Tina dramatically
jokes.punchline: The deflating detail about her riding a bike rather than dramatic exit
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment. The bike detail makes Carlton's grand gesture less impressive.

jokes.text: I might as well face it. I'm irresistible to women. Now I know what Tom Jones feels like.
jokes.setup: Carlton dealing with unwanted female attention
jokes.punchline: Carlton comparing himself to a sex symbol when he's clearly not
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton delusion. His complete lack of self-awareness makes the Tom Jones comparison hilarious.

jokes.text: Why don't you just do me like Kunta Kinte and chop off my foot?
jokes.setup: Will is grounded by Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic comparison to slavery punishment from Roots
jokes.explanation: Typical Will dramatic overreaction. The Roots reference is culturally specific and shows his tendency to melodrama.

jokes.text: Only after the El Presidente Platter.
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions Phil falling asleep after Mexican food
jokes.punchline: Phil's specific admission about which dish causes it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's specific knowledge of his food-induced naps. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I think the Jeffersons were still trying to move on up.
jokes.setup: Vivian referencing how long it's been since Phil went to the gym
jokes.punchline: Reference to old TV show to indicate extremely long time period
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference for dating Phil's gym absence. The Jeffersons reference works well for the era.

jokes.text: You'd believe that boy if he were to tell you he was a big rap star whose album just went platinum.
jokes.setup: Phil criticizing Vivian for being too trusting of Will
jokes.punchline: Phil unknowingly describing Will Smith's actual real-life career
jokes.explanation: Unintentional meta-humor since Will Smith actually was a rap star. Probably funnier in retrospect than intended.

jokes.text: Welcome to the briny deep, where none of our prices are ever too steep. We've got fresh fish. You can look in our tank. To get to the bathroom, make a left at the plank. Follow me.
jokes.setup: Will in pirate costume delivering restaurant greeting
jokes.punchline: Forced pirate-themed rhyming spiel with practical bathroom directions
jokes.explanation: Basic theme restaurant humor. The forced rhyming and pirate speak gets mild laughs but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: I think you'll be needing a booster seat, little boy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil has been criticizing the waiter's costume
jokes.punchline: Will's height-based insult disguised as helpful service
jokes.explanation: Basic height insult with service disguise. Will getting subtle revenge on Uncle Phil while in character.

jokes.text: Sorry, man, we ain't got no more.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil requests champagne after getting some for Vivian
jokes.punchline: Will's obvious lie about running out
jokes.explanation: Basic petty service revenge. Shows Will's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, sir, we've got all three flavors of Slim-Fast.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil can't decide what to drink
jokes.punchline: Will's weight-based menu suggestion
jokes.explanation: Basic fat joke disguised as menu option. Gets a reaction but relies on cheap target.

jokes.text: I like his eye patch, makes him look kind of dangerous. / You know, table tennis isn't exactly safe.
jokes.setup: Tina finds the pirate waiter dangerous-looking
jokes.punchline: Carlton's attempt to seem dangerous by referencing ping pong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton delusion callback. His attempt to make table tennis sound dangerous is hilariously desperate.

jokes.text: Tina, I doubt if it's real, and there's a French-fry in it.
jokes.setup: Tina compliments the waiter's beard
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical assessment of the fake beard's condition
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of romantic moment. Carlton's practical observation ruins Tina's fantasy about the pirate waiter.

jokes.text: I'm not a waiter. According to my uncle, I don't even have a job.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil calls Will the worst waiter ever
jokes.punchline: Will's identity reveal with bitter reference to Uncle Phil's earlier dismissal
jokes.explanation: Strong revelation moment that pays off the episode's setup. Good callback to Uncle Phil's earlier dismissal.

jokes.text: Carlton, you were my third love, and I'll never forget you... but I like Will now.
jokes.setup: Tina sees Will in pirate costume
jokes.punchline: Her immediate switch from Carlton to Will, casually mentioning he was only third
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tina's shallow infatuation. The 'third love' detail adds insult to Carlton's injury.

jokes.text: He's not the only guy who can dress like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Carlton losing Tina to Will in costume
jokes.punchline: Carlton's desperate plan to compete by dressing stupidly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's desperation and complete misunderstanding of attraction.

jokes.text: What did you say, pervert? / Oh, I'm sorry. I.... / Look, man, this is Treasure Island, okay? Now, Pleasure Island, that's a block down the street.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says he wants to be closer together with Will
jokes.punchline: Will's assumption about inappropriate intentions and geographic clarification
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding joke with Will jumping to wrong conclusion about Uncle Phil's intentions.

jokes.text: Yeah, wait till they make you bob for your lobster.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil feels foolish about the misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Will's description of another humiliating restaurant task
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist image of bobbing for lobster instead of apples. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: That's the biggest load of bull I've heard since I left the farm.
jokes.setup: Will gives speech about being a self-made man
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's folksy dismissal with farm reference
jokes.explanation: Good Uncle Phil line showing his background and cutting through Will's dramatics with folksy wisdom.

jokes.text: Of course, if I had my choice, I'd pick Ashley but....
jokes.setup: Will has just had touching moment about being like Uncle Phil's son
jokes.punchline: Will deflating the moment by saying he'd prefer to be like Ashley
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sentimental moment. Will can't resist undercutting the sweetness with a joke.

tmdb_overview: Will tries to juggle school and a new job in order to earn money for the homecoming party, at the same time Ashley's classmate Tina becomes interested in Carlton. Will starts becoming paranoid when he constantly is getting confused with Carlton.
